cards.title: The passive (continuous, perfect, past perfect, be going to, modals)
cards.subtitle: The passive (continuous, perfect, past perfect, be going to, modals)

sections.title: Form
sections.formula: be in the correct tense + past participle

sections.title: Tense transformations (active → passive)
rows: The chef is preparing the desserts.
The desserts **are being prepared** by the chef.
The school has organised the event.
The event **has been organised** by the school.
The workers were repairing the bridge.
The bridge **was being repaired** by the workers.
The police had closed the road.
The road **had been closed** by the police.
They are going to build a new stadium.
A new stadium **is going to be built**.
We can solve the problem easily.

sections.title: Be careful
items: Use **by** to introduce the person or thing that performs the action: The mural was painted **by** local artists.
Use **with** to introduce the tool or instrument used: The bread was cut **with** a sharp knife.
Leave out **by** or **with** when the agent or instrument is unknown or not relevant: The injured player was taken to hospital immediately.

exercises.title: A: Change into the passive
exercises.instructions: Rewrite each sentence in the passive.
exercises.studentInstruction: Rewrite each sentence in the passive.

items.displayPrompt: The manager is checking the documents.

cues: The manager is checking the documents.

items.answer: The documents are being checked by the manager.
acceptedAnswers: The documents are being checked by the manager.
items.explanation: Present continuous active → **are being checked** by the manager.

items.displayPrompt: The students have decorated the classroom.

cues: The students have decorated the classroom.

items.answer: The classroom has been decorated by the students.
acceptedAnswers: The classroom has been decorated by the students.
items.explanation: Present perfect active → **has been decorated** by the students.

items.displayPrompt: The engineers had completed the tunnel before winter.

cues: The engineers had completed the tunnel before winter.

items.answer: The tunnel had been completed by the engineers before winter.
acceptedAnswers: The tunnel had been completed by the engineers before winter.
items.explanation: Past perfect active → **had been completed** before winter.

items.displayPrompt: We can organise the event online.

cues: We can organise the event online.

items.answer: The event can be organised online.
acceptedAnswers: The event can be organised online.

exercises.exerciseTypeName: B: Complete with the correct passive form
exercises.title: B: Complete with the correct passive form
exercises.instructions: Use the verb in brackets to complete each sentence in the passive.
exercises.studentInstruction: Use the verb in brackets to complete each sentence in the passive.

items.sentence: The sports hall ...... (decorate) at the moment.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is at the moment, so the correct answer is "is being decorated".

wrongOptions.decorate: The bare infinitive (decorate) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Active voice puts the wrong focus here — passive needs be + past participle.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the subject and any time expressions before you conjugate — they decide tense and agreement.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Passive voice uses be + past participle — the focus is on the action, not the doer.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is already, so the correct answer is "have been reserved".

wrongOptions.been reserved: Missing auxiliary have — present perfect needs have + past participle.
wrongOptions.reserve: The bare infinitive (reserve) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple misses the link to now — present perfect needs have/has + past participle.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Time words like already, yet, since, and for are strong signals for present perfect.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Present perfect (have/has + past participle) links past actions to now or unfinished time.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is when we arrived (ongoing), so the correct answer is "were being installed".

wrongOptions.install: The bare infinitive (install) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check subject–verb agreement, tense, and whether the verb form matches the time clues in the sentence.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the subject and any time expressions before you conjugate — they decide tense and agreement.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Conjugate (install) to match the subject and the time clues in the sentence.

items.sentence: The decision ...... (approve) before the meeting started.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is before the meeting, so the correct answer is "had been approved".

wrongOptions.been approved: Without had, the earlier-past relationship is lost.
wrongOptions.approve: The bare infinitive (approve) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple only shows one past event — use had + past participle for the earlier action.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the subject and any time expressions before you conjugate — they decide tense and agreement.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Past perfect (had + past participle) shows the earlier of two past actions.

items.sentence: A new shopping centre ...... (open) next year.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is next year plan, so the correct answer is "is going to be opened".

wrongOptions.open: The bare infinitive (open) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check subject–verb agreement, tense, and whether the verb form matches the time clues in the sentence.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the subject and any time expressions before you conjugate — they decide tense and agreement.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Choose the future form that matches whether the sentence predicts, plans, or arranges.

items.sentence: Food ...... (not eat) in the science lab.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is rule/prohibition, so the correct answer is "must not be eaten".

wrongOptions.not eat: The bare infinitive (not eat) is not conjugated — adjust tense and person to fit the sentence.

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check subject–verb agreement, tense, and whether the verb form matches the time clues in the sentence.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Read the subject and any time expressions before you conjugate — they decide tense and agreement.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Modals and semi-modals express ability, obligation, permission, or possibility — match the nuance in the sentence.

exercises.title: C: by or with
exercises.instructions: Write by, with, or - (no word needed).
exercises.studentInstruction: Write by, with, or - (no word needed).

items.sentence: The article was written ...... a famous journalist.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is who wrote, so the correct answer is "by".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Relative clauses add detail about a noun — choose the pronoun that matches the antecedent.

items.sentence: The glass was cleaned ...... a special cloth.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is tool used, so the correct answer is "with".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Passive voice focuses on the action or object, not who performs it.

items.sentence: My wallet was stolen ...... last night.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is no agent or instrument, so the correct answer is "-".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Present perfect does not combine with finished past-time words like yesterday or last week.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Past simple is used for finished actions at a specific past time.

items.sentence: The vegetables were chopped ...... a large knife.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is instrument, so the correct answer is "with".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Passive voice focuses on the action or object, not who performs it.

items.sentence: The winning design was chosen ...... the judges.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is chosen by, so the correct answer is "by".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

exercises.title: D: Mixed passive forms
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct passive form for each sentence.
exercises.studentInstruction: Choose the correct passive form for each sentence.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The context points to "had been deleted" — before the manager arrived.

wrongOptions.A: "were deleted" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "had been deleted" is correct.
wrongOptions.were deleted: "were deleted" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "had been deleted" is correct.
wrongOptions.C: "have been deleted" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "had been deleted" is correct.
wrongOptions.have been deleted: "have been deleted" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "had been deleted" is correct.

explanationContent.usefulTip: Eliminate options that break grammar first, then check which remaining word fits the meaning.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "had been deleted" is the only option that matches both meaning and register in this sentence.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The context points to "is being designed" — at the moment.

wrongOptions.A: "was being designed" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "is being designed" is correct.
wrongOptions.was being designed: "was being designed" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "is being designed" is correct.
wrongOptions.B: "has been designed" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "is being designed" is correct.
wrongOptions.has been designed: "has been designed" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "is being designed" is correct.

explanationContent.usefulTip: Eliminate options that break grammar first, then check which remaining word fits the meaning.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "is being designed" is the only option that matches both meaning and register in this sentence.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The context points to "were asked" — past event.

wrongOptions.B: "had been asked" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "were asked" is correct.
wrongOptions.had been asked: "had been asked" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "were asked" is correct.
wrongOptions.C: "have been asked" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "were asked" is correct.
wrongOptions.have been asked: "have been asked" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "were asked" is correct.

explanationContent.usefulTip: Eliminate options that break grammar first, then check which remaining word fits the meaning.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "were asked" is the only option that matches both meaning and register in this sentence.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The context points to "was still being prepared" — when we entered (ongoing).

wrongOptions.A: "had still been prepared" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "was still being prepared" is correct.
wrongOptions.had still been prepared: "had still been prepared" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "was still being prepared" is correct.
wrongOptions.C: "is still being prepared" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "was still being prepared" is correct.
wrongOptions.is still being prepared: "is still being prepared" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "was still being prepared" is correct.

explanationContent.usefulTip: Eliminate options that break grammar first, then check which remaining word fits the meaning.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "was still being prepared" is the only option that matches both meaning and register in this sentence.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The context points to "will have been completed" — by next Friday.

wrongOptions.A: "will be completed" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "will have been completed" is correct.
wrongOptions.will be completed: "will be completed" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "will have been completed" is correct.
wrongOptions.B: "would have been completed" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "will have been completed" is correct.
wrongOptions.would have been completed: "would have been completed" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "will have been completed" is correct.

explanationContent.usefulTip: Eliminate options that break grammar first, then check which remaining word fits the meaning.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Future forms (will, going to, present continuous) depend on whether the sentence predicts, plans, or arranges.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The context points to "has been kept" — since Monday.

wrongOptions.A: "was kept" does not express the present-perfect link to now that the sentence requires.
wrongOptions.was kept: "was kept" does not express the present-perfect link to now that the sentence requires.
wrongOptions.C: "had been kept" does not express the present-perfect link to now that the sentence requires.
wrongOptions.had been kept: "had been kept" does not express the present-perfect link to now that the sentence requires.

explanationContent.usefulTip: Time expressions like for, since, already, and yet are strong present-perfect signals.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Present perfect links past actions to now — look for markers like for, since, already, yet, or ever.
